name: 07_negative_vol_degenerate_clamps_to_upper
description: Degenerate input (negative symphony_vol) produces a positive raw; the upper clamp pins the result at VWAP_BLEED_ARM_MAX = -0.5. The math is well-defined even on bogus inputs because the clamp window itself is always negative.
derivation: raw = -(-1.0 * 1.5) = 1.5 (positive). min(-0.5, 1.5) = -0.5. max(-3.0, -0.5) = -0.5. Even with a positive raw, the clamp window [-3.0, -0.5] forces the output to its upper bound. Output is still <= 0 (the bleed-threshold sign invariant).
